release_notes: Adds a tiny whimsy feature: after a few qualifying outgoing Ready signals (4 to 8 sends, with at least 10 minutes between each qualifying send so rapid clicks can't game it), a black-outline stick figure pops out from behind the Ready button, wanders to a random spot on the app, performs an 8-12 second whimsical bit (looks surprised, reads a newspaper, stretches, rides a horse, does jumping jacks, takes a nap, lifts weights, dances, cartwheels, or holds a yoga tree pose) and returns. Only shows on the sending workstation. New module ready_animations.py contains the animation framework, the 10 animations, and an AnimationTrigger state machine that enforces the spacing rule. A separate ready_animation_picker.py script (run with `python3 ready_animation_picker.py`) lets the user pick a stick-figure character (plain / wavy hair / top hat / mohawk / glasses / long beard / bow tie), enable/disable individual animations, and preview them — the preview uses the same Toplevel + Canvas + draw functions the live trigger uses, so what the user sees in the picker is exactly what plays in the main app. Preferences (animation_preferences dict and animation_character string) live in the existing chairside_ready_alert_config.json file. Cross-platform: Windows uses -transparentcolor for transparent overlay, macOS uses -transparent. Triggers are best-effort and never disrupt actual Ready sending.
